claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 6 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 16 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child in local authority care for 9 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in approved education
  - Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05); Child in local authority care for 9 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have two children I'd like to ask about - Alex, who's 6, and Blake, who's 16.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'd like to learn more about your situation with Alex and Blake.

Let's start with the basics: **Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK at the moment. I'm living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, this may affect your eligibility, but there are some circumstances where you can still claim Child Benefit.

**Which country are you currently living in, and are you or your partner a Crown servant (such as a diplomat or member of the armed forces posted abroad)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm living in Spain at the moment, and no, neither myself nor my partner are Crown servants or anything like that. We're not diplomats or in the armed forces.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

**Are you or your partner working for a UK-based employer or organization while in Spain, or do you have any other connection to the UK such as continuing to pay UK National Insurance contributions?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, we don't work for a UK-based employer and we're not paying UK National Insurance contributions at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I need to ask a few more questions to fully assess your situation.

**Do you or your partner have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, we don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Let me ask one more important question:

**Are you and your children currently registered or residing in Spain permanently, or are you planning to return to live in the UK in the near future?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: We're currently living in Spain, but I'm not sure why that matters - I'm just trying to find out if I can claim Child Benefit for my children. Can you tell me what the situation is?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. I have enough information now to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex and Blake.

Based on the information you've provided, I need to explain the key rule that affects your situation:

**Residence Requirement:** To qualify for Child Benefit, you normally must live in the UK. There are exceptions for people living abroad, such as Crown servants (diplomats, armed forces personnel) or in certain specific countries with reciprocal agreements, but unfortunately, simply living in Spain without being a Crown servant or having specific UK employment/National Insurance connections does not qualify you for Child Benefit.

Since you're living in Spain and are not a Crown servant, not working for a UK employer, and don't have settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme that would create a right to reside in the UK, you would not meet the basic eligibility requirement of living in the UK.

Let me provide my formal assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Assessment Complete**

I'm sorry to inform you that you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake at this time.

**The reason:** The main requirement for Child Benefit is that you must live in the UK. Since you're living in Spain and don't fall into any of the special exception categories (such as being a Crown servant, working for a UK employer abroad, or having certain EU settlement status), you don't meet this fundamental requirement.

If your circumstances change and you move back to the UK in the future, you would be able to apply for Child Benefit at that time, assuming you meet the other eligibility criteria.

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old, which is under 16, so would normally meet the age requirement. However, the claimant does not meet the basic residence requirement for Child Benefit. The claimant lives in Spain and is not a Crown servant, does not work for a UK employer, does not pay UK National Insurance, and does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. Since the claimant does not live in the UK and does not fall into any of the exceptions for living abroad, they are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 16 years old. While children aged 16-19 can qualify for Child Benefit if they stay in approved education or training, the claimant does not meet the basic residence requirement for Child Benefit. The claimant lives in Spain and is not a Crown servant, does not work for a UK employer, does not pay UK National Insurance, and does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. Since the claimant does not live in the UK and does not fall into any of the exceptions for living abroad, they are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has two children (Alex, age 6, and Blake, age 16) but lives in Spain. The fundamental requirement for Child Benefit is that the claimant must live in the UK. While there are exceptions for those living abroad (such as Crown servants or those in certain countries with specific arrangements), the claimant does not fall into any of these exception categories. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for either child due to not meeting the residence requirement.
